title: dalbergia maritima subsp. maritima
intro: Dalbergia maritima subsp. maritima, also known as the Cape ebony, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the coastal regions of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Dalbergia maritima subsp. maritima include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
